blockers.evidence: Verified local identity manifest now includes public brand, legal entity, street address, telephone, email, opening hours and closed days; Dentist/LocalBusiness node binds address, telephone, email and openingHoursSpecification.

metadataBase, production noindex policy, canonical alternates, OpenGraph and Twitter are present. Missing evidence remains for title templates, explicit icon/app-icon metadata and route-specific OG image policy.

Dentist/LocalBusiness schema now includes NAP, opening hours, areaServed and employee @id references. Missing evidence remains for geo coordinates, sameAs, image/logo, priceRange and hasMap/GBP identifiers.
Treatment route emits buildTreatmentSchemaGraph with WebPage, Service and BreadcrumbList; Service provider references canonical dentist @id.
FAQ UI/data exists across section layouts, but no FAQPage schema emitter exists. Article/Video/Image/Offer schema remains intentionally absent unless governed source data exists.
Visible rendering is resolved at 100% for 11 target routes, but priority packets still have approved_for_ai/voice/chatbot/schema false and clinician_review_required true.
Team registry now has 5 GDC numbers and 5 verified schema members. Remaining gap: explicit reviewer linkage from every launch-critical treatment answer to named clinicians.
Answer surfaces use cautious clinical-boundary language, but priority packets remain clinician_review_required true and not approved for AI/schema/chatbot/voice.

Priority treatment routes, packets, questions and visible answers exist. Content maturity still has QA warnings for overlong answers and cannibalisation clusters requiring final editorial/intent review.
Chatbot alignment registry exists with runtimeMutation=false. Runtime sync was intentionally not changed; priority packets remain not approved_for_chatbot.
No IndexNow key file, endpoint route or submission script evidence found.
No committed Lighthouse, Core Web Vitals or axe/accessibility audit output found for the current build. Build/verify evidence exists but not performance/accessibility scoring.
